summary: Small build-system fix that updated the top-level Makefile linker flags to include `-fp hard` and `-nodefaults`, matching existing compiler configuration and suppressing linker warnings.
what_changed: No source code or decompilation units were changed; the PR only touched the project build configuration.
The added linker flags mirror options already present in `CFLAGS`, where `-fp hard` and `-nodefaults` were already used for compilation.
smart_moves: Kept the fix minimal and localized to the linker configuration instead of changing compiler flags or source files.
Aligned linker behavior with the existing PowerPC/Gekko hard-float and no-default-libraries assumptions used by the compiler flags.
Preserved the existing map-file generation flag `-map $(MAP)` while adding only the warning-relevant options.
decomp_lessons: For this project, warning cleanup may require keeping `CFLAGS` and `LDFLAGS` semantically consistent, especially around target ABI options such as hard-float mode.
Build-system flags are part of matching infrastructure: inconsistent compiler/linker settings can produce noisy warnings even when object code is otherwise unaffected.
Small Makefile-only fixes should be recorded because they encode assumptions future decomp work depends on, such as `-nodefaults` and `-fp hard` for the target toolchain.
naming_conventions: The linker map path is referenced through the existing `$(MAP)` variable.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function-matching tactic is shown in this PR.
The relevant matching-support tactic is build-environment consistency: link with hard-float and no default libraries to match the configured Gekko compile target.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later Makefile changes involving `LDFLAGS`, `-fp hard`, or `-nodefaults`.
Search for linker warning fixes in other early PRs.
Check whether toolchain documentation explains why `-fp hard` and `-nodefaults` are required at both compile and link stages.
